reviews.review_msg: In my four years of school this was the most interesting class I have taken. There is a lot of reading - but it is very interesting. Ian is also a very bright person who really helps break down the ideas of the great thinkers of our time into something an undergrad can understand. I recommend a class with Ian if you get a chance, great guy.

The topics and readings were very interesting, and the class discussions were great. However, there is some sense that topics not necessarily relate to one another (and he does not do a great job helping there). His grading is really unclear despite the rubric. The feedback is minimal and appears unimportant, yet his grading is tough

Great class! Il Professore is top notch and will challenge you to do your best. He teaches independent critical thinking and writing through Italian cinema. If you buy into the process, do the work and participate in discussion, he will reward you. Office hour feedback is great, way better than written comments. If you rely on AI, it won't help.

He is an amazing professor. He's very passionate, and he's very willing to help you. Pick Paul if you don't have a busy semester, and if you like writing.

Don't take his course as an elective. His seminar is 4-hour long, it's hard to keep focus since it's just all talking. No written feedback, only during office hours. Stressful as non-hum major

Jamie teaches the sort of class that you would never cheat in because you would feel so bad about doing it. Would give you an extension if an assignment was due on your birthday. 

He is also clearly passionate about teaching history and gives engaging, interesting lectures. 

Tends to get sick semi-often... check your email before class :')

By far the best professor I have had at SFU. Great lecturer, very passionate, and his workload is very manageable. Participation is a must, but the course content lends itself to discussion, so keep up with the readings and stay engaged. Cannot recommend taking courses with Jamie enough.

Professor Horncastle is one of the best professors I've had at SFU. His lectures are interesting, and you can tell he is passionate about what he teaches! All assignments are provided with clear instructions, and he always provides great feedbacks. He is friendly and open to questions or concerns!
